mission.title: Install and Configure Alertmanager (Prometheus) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Alertmanager is a key component of the Prometheus ecosystem that handles alerts sent by client applications. It manages alert deduplication, grouping, and routing to various notification channels, making it essential for effective alert management in your observability stack.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Prometheus community Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the Alertmanager chart.
```bash
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Alertmanager using Helm
steps.description:
Install Alertmanager with a specific version and create a namespace for it.
```bash
helm install alertmanager prometheus-community/alertmanager --version 0.31.1 --namespace monitoring --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Alertmanager pod is running successfully in the monitoring namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace monitoring
```

steps.title: Configure Alertmanager
steps.description:
Create a configuration file for Alertmanager and apply it. Replace `<your_alertmanager_config.yml>` with your actual configuration file path.
```bash
kubectl create configmap alertmanager-config --from-file=<your_alertmanager_config.yml> --namespace monitoring
kubectl patch deployment alertmanager --namespace monitoring --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args", "value": ["--config.file=/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml"]}]'
```

steps.title: Expose Alertmanager service
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access Alertmanager from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/alertmanager --namespace monitoring 9093:9093
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Alertmanager pod running in the monitoring namespace and allow you to access the Alertmanager UI at http://localhost:9093. You should also have a working configuration applied to manage alerts.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

kubectl create configmap alertmanager-config --from-file=<your_alertmanager_config.yml> --namespace monitoring
kubectl patch deployment alertmanager --namespace monitoring --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/args", "value": ["--config.file=/etc/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml"]}]'

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
